question.text: The aggregate ceiling of NRI investment under PIS can be raised from 10 per cent to 24 per cent by:

answer.text: Obtaining prior approval from the Reserve Bank of India
Passing a resolution by the Board of Directors followed by a special resolution of the shareholders
Filing a notification with the Ministry of Finance

explanation.text: **The Logic:** The aggregate paid-up value of shares/convertible debentures purchased by all NRIs cannot exceed 10% of paid-up capital. This aggregate ceiling of 10% can be raised to 24% by passing a resolution by its Board of Directors followed by a special resolution of the shareholders. **The Trap:** This is a company-level decision requiring both board resolution AND shareholder special resolution. No RBI or SEBI approval is needed for this increase. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** The same mechanism applies for FPIs - the aggregate FPI limit of 24% can also be increased to the sectoral cap through similar board and shareholder resolutions.
